result.summary: Elastic exposes multiple authenticated developer surfaces: REST APIs for Elastic Cloud, Cloud Billing, Cloud Connected, Cloud Serverless, Elasticsearch, Kibana, Elasticsearch Serverless, Kibana Serverless, Elastic Cloud Enterprise, an Agent Builder MCP endpoint, and the ecctl CLI.
result.description: Elastic provides search, analytics, observability, and security products built around Elasticsearch and Kibana, offered as self-managed software and cloud services. Its platform includes hosted, serverless, and enterprise control-plane offerings for managing deployments and projects.

elastic_cloud_api_key.label: Elastic Cloud API key
elastic_cloud_api_key.setup: Sign in to the [Elastic Cloud Console](https://cloud.elastic.co), open **Organization** from your avatar menu, then on the **API keys** tab click **Create API key**. Only organization owners can create and manage these keys. Use the key in the `Authorization` header as `ApiKey <key>`. See [Elastic Cloud API keys](https://www.elastic.co/docs/deploy-manage/api-keys/elastic-cloud-api-keys).

elasticsearch_api_key.label: Elasticsearch API key
elasticsearch_api_key.setup: In Kibana, open the **API keys** management page (or **Help menu → Connection details → API key** for a quick personal key), then click **Create API key** and configure its privileges. These keys are used for stack-versioned Elasticsearch and Kibana APIs. Use the key in the `Authorization` header as `ApiKey <key>`. See [Elasticsearch API keys](https://www.elastic.co/docs/deploy-manage/api-keys/elasticsearch-api-keys).

serverless_project_api_key.label: Serverless project API key
serverless_project_api_key.setup: In a Serverless project's Kibana UI, open **API keys**, click **Create API key**, and copy the encoded key. These keys are tied to a single serverless project. Use the key in the `Authorization` header as `ApiKey <key>`. See [Serverless project API keys](https://www.elastic.co/docs/deploy-manage/api-keys/serverless-project-api-keys).

ece_api_key.label: Elastic Cloud Enterprise API key
ece_api_key.setup: Sign in to your Elastic Cloud Enterprise Cloud UI, then go to **Profile → Settings → API Keys** and choose **Generate API key**. Copy the generated key and send it in API requests in the `Authorization` header as `ApiKey <key>`. See [Elastic Cloud Enterprise API keys](https://www.elastic.co/docs/deploy-manage/api-keys/elastic-cloud-enterprise-api-keys).

ece_bearer_token.label: Elastic Cloud Enterprise bearer token
ece_bearer_token.setup: Use your Elastic Cloud Enterprise UI username and password to obtain a JWT from the ECE login endpoint by POSTing credentials to `https://$COORDINATOR_HOST:12443/api/v1/users/auth/_login`, then use the returned token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. See [Elastic Cloud Enterprise API keys](https://www.elastic.co/docs/deploy-manage/api-keys/elastic-cloud-enterprise-api-keys) under bearer token authentication.

surfaces.name: Observability Intake Serverless API
surfaces.notes: Docs show a placeholder server host; the real base URL is tenant- or deployment-specific.
